name: Turkmenistan
avoid: June–August desert heat around Darvaza, Merv and Konye-Urgench where midday sightseeing becomes draining fast
Last-minute planning — most tourists need a letter of invitation plus a pre-arranged licensed guide and driver
Casual photography near ministries, checkpoints and some public buildings in Ashgabat where security can intervene quickly

cities.name: Ashgabat
cities.notes: The white-marble capital is the easiest place for visa formalities, banks, museums, Nisa day trips and the broadest hotel choice.

cities.name: Darvaza (Gates of Hell)
cities.notes: Remote Karakum Desert gas crater best done as an overnight guided camp with a 4x4, sunset arrival and early-morning departure.

cities.name: Merv
cities.notes: Use Mary as the hotel base for the vast UNESCO ruins of Ancient Merv, Sultan Sanjar Mausoleum and nearby desert archaeology.

cities.name: Konye-Urgench
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed Khwarazm capital of minarets and mausoleums, usually visited as a guided day trip from Dashoguz.

itinerary.sim: TM CELL or Altyn Asyr setup is easiest through a hotel or guide in Ashgabat; expect roughly ₹700–₹1,400 for a short tourist data pack, and keep offline maps because coverage and speeds drop quickly outside the cities.

Ashgabat.name: Ashgabat
Ashgabat.note: Two days covers the capital highlights plus Old Nisa; keep a third only if you want slower museum time or extra visa-buffer space.

activities.name: National Museum of Turkmenistan
Turkmen Carpet Museum
Arch of Neutrality observation stop and Independence Park drive-by
Alem Cultural and Entertainment Center Ferris wheel complex
Evening taxi ride along Magtymguly Avenue and the white-marble government quarter
Old Nisa archaeological site
activities.tip: Recent visitor reports put the ticket around 10–20 TMT, so carry small cash for the guard booth.

activities.name: Turkmenbashi Ruhy Mosque at Gypjak
Wedding Palace and Yyldyz Hotel hilltop panorama stop
Berkarar shopping complex or Russian Bazaar for snacks and ATM backup
Tolkuchka Bazaar outing with your guide for carpets and silverwork
Spa or pool downtime at Oguzkent Hotel or Yyldyz Hotel
Keep this day for flight shifts, migration formalities or a slower city drive

Merv.name: Merv
Merv.note: One long day works if you start early from Mary with a guide; a second day helps only if you want Mary museums or a gentler pace in the ruins.

activities.name: Ancient Merv archaeological park ticket
activities.tip: Current reports usually place entry around 10–20 TMT; photography can be charged separately at roughly ₹1,185.

activities.name: Sultan Sanjar Mausoleum
Erk Kala, Gyaur Kala and Shahriyar Ark circuit with guide
Greater and Lesser Kyz Kala adobe fortresses
Round-trip taxi or guided transfer from Mary
Mary History Museum or local bazaar walk before the next domestic flight
Short tea stop and souvenir shopping in central Mary

Darvaza (Gates of Hell).name: Darvaza (Gates of Hell)
Darvaza (Gates of Hell).note: Treat Darvaza as an overnight desert camp: arrive before sunset, stay for the crater glow, and leave at sunrise before the heat builds.

activities.name: Guided 4x4 transfer across the Karakum Desert via Erbent village
Darvaza Gas Crater sunset walk and crater-rim viewing
Overnight yurt or tent stay at Ayan Yurt Camp / similar Darvaza camp
activities.tip: Recent camp listings and traveler reports put simple overnight camping with dinner and breakfast around $30–$60 per person.

activities.name: Sunrise visit to the nearby water crater before departure
activities.cost: Included if your driver adds the stop

Konye-Urgench.name: Konye-Urgench
Konye-Urgench.note: Most travelers do Konye-Urgench as a guided day trip from Dashoguz because the site is spread out but the city itself has limited tourist infrastructure.

activities.name: Kutlug Timur Minaret and Turabek Khanum Mausoleum complex
Najm ad-Din al-Kubra Mausoleum
Il Arslan Mausoleum and caravan-route ruins
Round-trip Dashoguz taxi or guided transfer
Dashoguz city rest day before the domestic flight back to Ashgabat
Leave extra time for checkpoint delays and flight reschedules

connections.method: Turkmenistan Airlines domestic flight from Ashgabat to Mary plus short taxi to hotel
Private guided overland transfer from Mary through the Karakum; this is usually bundled by your agency rather than sold as public transport
Continue north by 4x4 to Dashoguz, then taxi onward for the UNESCO site visit
Taxi back to Dashoguz and Turkmenistan Airlines domestic flight to Ashgabat

extras: Most tourists need a letter of invitation and a licensed guide/driver for the full trip; only short transit-visa travelers move more independently.
Best weather is usually April–May and September–October; Darvaza nights can still feel cold, so pack a fleece even when Ashgabat is warm.
Carry cash in Turkmen manat for museum tickets, site guards and rural tea stops because cards are rarely useful outside major hotels.
Internet is heavily filtered and often slow, so preload translations, maps and hotel confirmations before leaving Ashgabat.
Photography around ministries, military sites and checkpoints can trigger trouble quickly; ask your guide before taking city shots.
If you only have 5–6 days, keep Ashgabat, Merv and Darvaza, then add Konye-Urgench only if the Dashoguz flight schedule lines up.
